task_completion.description: Open VeraCrypt and navigate to the Benchmark tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The VeraCrypt 'VeraCrypt Volume Creation Wizard' or main window is open, and the 'VeraCrypt Benchmark' dialog window is visible.

task_completion.description: Set the Buffer Size to 100 MB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the VeraCrypt Benchmark dialog, the 'Buffer Size' dropdown or input field is set to '100 MB'.
The list in the VeraCrypt Benchmark dialog is populated with encryption algorithms and their corresponding Encryption and Decryption speeds, indicating the benchmark has been run.

task_completion.description: Create a text report at /home/ga/Documents/benchmark_report.txt with the required table columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'benchmark_report.txt' (saved in /home/ga/Documents/). The text contains a table with headers for 'Algorithm Name', 'Encryption Speed (MB/s)', 'Decryption Speed (MB/s)', and 'Mean Speed'.

task_completion.description: The report contains the required algorithms and valid throughput values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report lists AES, Serpent, Twofish, Camellia, and Kuznyechik. The speed values are numeric, and there are at least 10 valid throughput values between 10.0 and 50000.0 MB/s.

task_completion.description: Explicitly state the Fastest and Slowest algorithms based on Mean Speed at the bottom of the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At the bottom of the text report, there are explicit statements matching 'Fastest Algorithm: [Name] ([Speed] MB/s)' and 'Slowest Algorithm: [Name] ([Speed] MB/s)'. The identified algorithms and speeds must correctly correspond to the highest and lowest Mean Speeds in the table.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the VeraCrypt GUI benchmark tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the VeraCrypt Benchmark GUI actively running or having completed the test, rather than the agent just typing fabricated numbers into a text editor.

integrity.description: The speeds in the text report match the actual benchmark results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The encryption and decryption speeds typed into the text report should match the numbers displayed in the VeraCrypt Benchmark GUI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal script to generate a fake report bypassing the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing python or bash scripts echoing fake benchmark data directly into the report file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected report output path is /home/ga/Documents/benchmark_report.txt. The report must contain the 5 standard algorithms: AES, Serpent, Twofish, Camellia, Kuznyechik. There must be at least 10 valid throughput numeric values in the report. Valid throughput values must be strictly between 10.0 MB/s and 50000.0 MB/s.
